title: Ensure 'Minimum Inbound TLS Version' is set to '1.2' or  higher

input_row.title: Ensure 'Minimum Inbound TLS Version' is set to '1.2' or  higher
input_row.description: The TLS (Transport Layer Security) protocol secures the transmission of data over the internet using standard encryption technology. App Service deployment slots use TLS 1.2 for the Minimum Inbound TLS Version by default and allow for the use of TLS versions 1.0, 1.1, and 1.3. NIST strongly suggests the use of TLS 1.2 and recommends the adoption of TLS 1.3.
input_row.rationale: TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are outdated and vulnerable to security risks. Since TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3 provide enhanced security and improved performance, it is highly recommended to use TLS 1.2 or higher whenever possible.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings pane, under Platform settings, ensure that Minimum Inbound TLS Version is set to 1.2 or higher. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each app and deployment slot. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list apps: az webapp list For each app, run the following command to list deployment slots: az webapp deployment slot list --resource-group --name For each deployment slot, run the following command to get the TLS version setting: az resource show --name web --resource-group -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type config --parent sites//slots/ --query properties.minTlsVersion Ensure that "1.2" or higher is returned. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 4ee5b817-627a-435a-8932-116193268172 - Name: 'App Service app slots should use the latest TLS version'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings pane, under Platform settings, click the drop-down next to Minimum Inbound TLS Version and select 1.2 or higher. 7. Click Save. 8. Click Continue. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each app and deployment slot requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each deployment slot requiring remediation, run the following command to update the TLS version: az resource update --name web --resource-group -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type config --parent sites//slots/ --set properties.minTlsVersion=<1.2|1.3>
input_row.impact: Using the latest TLS version may affect compatibility with clients and backend services.
input_row.default_value: By default, TLS version is set to 1.2.

step1_initial_assessment:
Given the analysis of the provided compliance control related to Azure, and its adaptation to AWS, here is the determined audit approach:

**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_elastic_load_balancing_minimum_tls_version_enforced

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would check the following:

- **Resources to Enumerate:** All Elastic Load Balancers across the AWS account in the specified regions.

- **AWS API Calls / Boto3 SDK Methods:**
  - Use `boto3.client('elbv2')` to interact with the Elastic Load Balancing service.
  - Specifically, the `describe_listeners()` method will be employed to retrieve settings for listeners associated with the load balancers.
  
- **AWS CLI Commands:**
  - The command `aws elbv2 describe-listeners --load-balancer-arn <LoadBalancerArn>` can be used to get the configurations of the listeners.

- **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
  - Check for the TLS version settings under the listener described in the above API call.
  - The specific property to check would likely be related to the `SslPolicy` attribute of the listener.

- **Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
  - If the `SslPolicy` configuration is set to a predefined TLS version that includes "ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS-1-2-2017-01" or higher, then the check would pass.
  - If the policy indicates a TLS version below 1.2, the check would fail.

Example Check:
```python
def check_tls_version(listener_arn):
    response = elbv2.describe_listeners(ListenerArns=[listener_arn])
    for listener in response['Listeners']:
        if 'SslPolicy' in listener:
            if "TLS-1-2" in listener['SslPolicy']:
                return True
    return False
```

This approach takes into account the necessary automation capabilities provided by AWS APIs and focuses on specific configuration checks related to TLS version enforcement in the context of Elastic Load Balancers.
